question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a cascade where a network partition causes BlockStreamException to be thrown, which is caught and re-thrown as a new BlockStreamException(Throwable), creating a growing exception chain on each retry cycle that eventually causes OutOfMemoryError due to unbounded exception object accumulation in the heap?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message, Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockNode.onError() method's non-atomic check-then-act on the errors AtomicInteger (errors.incrementAndGet() >= maxAttempts then active=false) by sending concurrent error-triggering requests from multiple connections, causing the node to be marked inactive after fewer errors than maxAttempts due to a race condition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockNode.getBlockRange() catches all Exception types and returns EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE without incrementing the error counter, to repeatedly cause exceptions in getBlockRange() without triggering node deactivation, while simultaneously causing exceptions in streamBlocks() to deactivate nodes, creating an asymmetric partition state?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a block stream that triggers BlockStreamException('Incorrect first block item case ' + firstItemCase) with a firstItemCase value containing special characters, causing the exception message to be logged with unescaped content that corrupts structured log parsers and blinds monitoring systems during a partition event?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message, Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StreamFileReaderException (extends ImporterException extends MirrorNodeException) is thrown with a Throwable cause, and that the cause is not sanitized before being included in log output, to exfiltrate internal file paths or cloud storage bucket names during a partition-induced read failure?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a FieldInaccessibleException (extends ImporterException extends MirrorNodeException) to be thrown during block parsing by crafting a block with a field that triggers a reflection access failure, and if this exception is not distinguished from a network partition exception, cause the importer to incorrectly apply partition-recovery logic (e.g., node failover) instead of rejecting the malformed block?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockAssembler.timeout() method returning a negative value when the stopwatch has exceeded the timeout duration, causing BlockingClientCall.read() to be called with a negative timeout, which may be interpreted as an infinite wait, hanging the importer thread indefinitely and simulating a partition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MirrorNodeException extends RuntimeException (which implements Serializable via serialVersionUID) to send a crafted serialized MirrorNodeException subclass over a Java RMI or JMX channel, triggering a deserialization vulnerability that leads to arbitrary code execution on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/exception/MirrorNodeException.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: MirrorNodeException(String message, Throwable throwable)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a ParserException (extends ImporterException extends MirrorNodeException) by submitting a block with a timestamp that causes a long overflow, and if this exception is caught by a partition-recovery handler instead of a parse-error handler, cause the importer to retry the same malformed block indefinitely, exhausting resources?
